LAW AND MOTION 302, "IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS CURRENTLY CALENDARED IN DEPARTMENTS 301/302 ARE HEREBY VACATED. WHEN THE COURT RESUMES NORMAL OPERATIONS, THE PRESIDING JUDGE WILL ANNOUNCE PROCEDURES FOR SETTING HEARING DATES FOR THOSE CASES WHICH HAVE BEEN FULLY BRIEFED. OPPOSITIONS WILL BE DUE BASED ON THE RESCHEDULED HEARING DATE, BUT NOTHING PRECLUDES A PARTY FROM FILING AN OPPOSITION EARLIER." (302)

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, JUN-12-2020 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. THE COURT TAKES THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: RICHARD B. IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS IN DEPARTMENT 302 FILED BEFORE JUNE 1, 2020 ARE OFF CALENDAR PURSUANT TO THE COURT'S GENERAL ORDERS. TO RE-SCHEDULE THE HEARING, PLEASE REFER TO THE PUBLIC NOTICE TO RESTORE SERVICES ISSUED ON MAY 27, 2020 AVAILABLE ON THE COURT'S WEBSITE AT HTTPS://SFSUPERIORCOURT.ORG/. (302/EPS)

ASSIGNED TO ARBITRATION FOR DEC-09-2019. THE OCT-09-2019 CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-27-2019 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND HAVE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION DEEMED ADMITTED HELD ON DEC-27-2019, IN DEPT. 302, IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. WITHIN FIVE (5) COURT DAYS AFTER SERVICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER, PLAINTIFF SHALL SERVE UPON DEFENDANT, CODE-COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO: 1) DEFENDANT'S FORM INTERROGATORIES-GENERAL, SET ONE, TO PLAINTIFF; 2) DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, TO PLAINTIFF; AND 3) DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, TO PLAINTIFF. DEFENDANT'S REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS, SET ONE, TO PLAINTIFF DID NOT COMPLY WITH C.C.P. 2033.060(E). THUS, THE MOTION TO DEEM ADMISSIONS ADMITTED IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: WILLIAM LYNN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302/DISCOVERY.

Matter argued and submitted by the parties. The Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

Defendant City And County Of San Franciscos Re-Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Jun-05-2020 Continued To Jun-12-20 At 09:31 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Raymond Rollan, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-3800, office of the City Attorney for Defendant CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

RULING - A R G U E D. Counsel informs the Court Plaintiff in Pro Per came to the Court house, cannot afford Court Call, that it is too late to obtain a fee waiver and that Plaintiff is contesting the tentative ruling. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

Continued to 6/12/20 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302. Counsel to notify Plaintiff to appear in person on 6/12/20. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Mar-27-2020 Off Calendar

ARBITRATOR JURISDICTION CALENDAR LIST
Off Calendar Nov-02-2021 Off Calendar

Defendant City And County Of San Franciscos Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted
Off Calendar Dec-27-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
William Jackson, in pro per, Tel. 916-856-4163, for plaintiffs;
No other appearances.

Ruling: The matter is discussed. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted is granted in part and denied in part. Within five (5) court days after service of entry of this order, Plaintiff shall serve upon Defendant, code-compliant responses to: 1) Defendant's Form Interrogatories-General, Set One, to Plaintiff; 2) Defendant's Special Interrogatories, Set One, to Plaintiff; and 3) Defendant's Request for Production of Documents, Set One, to Plaintiff. Defendant's Requests for Admissions, Set One, to Plaintiff did not comply with C.C.P. 2033.060(e). Thus, the motion to deem admissions admitted is denied. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302/Discovery.
